metadata.title: Explore the Water Cycle
metadata.summary: Tap the labelled regions of a water-cycle diagram to reveal what happens at each stage.
metadata.subject: science
metadata.learningObjective: Describe evaporation, condensation, precipitation and collection as connected stages of the water cycle.

components.text: Open every marker, then use the final sentence of each explanation to describe how one stage leads into the next.

components.prompt: Tap each marker, then explain how that stage leads to the next one.
components.altText: A landscape diagram showing the sea, rising water vapour, clouds, rain over hills and a river returning water to the sea.

hotspots.label: Evaporation
hotspots.reveal: Energy from the Sun changes liquid water at the surface into water vapour, which rises into the air.

hotspots.label: Condensation
hotspots.reveal: As water vapour rises and cools, it changes into tiny liquid droplets that gather to form clouds.

hotspots.label: Precipitation
hotspots.reveal: When droplets in a cloud become heavy enough, water falls to Earth as rain, snow, sleet or hail.

hotspots.label: Collection
hotspots.reveal: Water gathers in rivers, lakes, the ground and the sea before the cycle continues.
